mission.title: n8n: n8n is taking a lot of RAM memory
mission.description: n8n is taking a lot of RAM memory. This issue affects 5+ users.

steps.title: Identify n8n analyze symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your n8n deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n -n n8n --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review n8n configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant n8n configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl get configmap -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=n8n
```
The n8n process is taking a lot of RAM memory, in this case 1.1 GB, and 41.7 GB of virtual memory.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. Install n8n using Docker/Portainer
2.

steps.title: Apply the fix for n8n is taking a lot of RAM memory
steps.description:
## Summary

This PR addresses issue #7939 where users report significant memory growth when using Python in Code nodes. The solution implements a Worker approach that completely resolves the memory leak pattern.

Memory usage comparison charts:
```yaml
my_list = [0] * 1000000
return {"a": my_list}
```

steps.title: Confirm n8n is taking a lot of RAM memory is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n -n n8n --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n n8n --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
## Summary

This PR addresses issue #7939 where users report significant memory growth when using Python in Code nodes. The solution implements a Worker approach that completely resolves the memory leak pattern.

# n8n with persistent volume Postgres
# https://raw.githubusercontent.com/n8n-io/n8n/master/docker/compose/withPostgres/docker-compose.yml

version: '3.8'

volumes:
  db_data:
  main_data:

services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:11
    restart: always
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_USER=${POSTGRES_USER}
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}
      - POSTGRES_DB=${POSTGRES_DB}
      - POSTGRES_NON_ROOT_USER=${POSTGRES_NON_ROOT_USER}
      - POSTGRES_NON_ROOT_PASSWORD=${POSTGRES_NON_ROOT_PASSWORD}
    volumes:
      - db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      - ${N8N_LOCAL_FILES}/n8n-config/init-data.sh:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init-data.sh
    healthcheck:
      test: ['CMD-SHELL', 'pg_isready -h localhost -U ${POSTGRES_USER} -d ${POSTGRES_DB}']
      interval: 5s
      timeou

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with n8n installed or the issue environment reproducible.
